name: Aconine
description: Aconine is an Aconitum-related diterpenoid alkaloid generally discussed within aconitum toxicology rather than therapeutic pharmacology. Direct human evidence is limited.
effects: Aconine is a hydrolysis-related aconitum alkaloid discussed mainly in toxicity context.
Compared with aconitine, available evidence remains sparse and largely non-clinical.
contraindications: Avoid self-administration of aconitum-derived concentrates due to narrow safety margins across related alkaloids.
interactions: Compound-specific interaction evidence is limited; class-based cardiotoxic risk warrants caution with antiarrhythmic-active drugs.

sources.title: Research progress of aconitine toxicity and forensic analysis of aconitine poisoning.

summary: Aconine is an Aconitum-related diterpenoid alkaloid generally discussed within aconitum toxicology rather than therapeutic pharmacology. Direct human evidence is limited.
